programme.title: Matter of Fact
programme.desc: This week Matter of Fact travels to Los Angeles to see how a shelter organization is meeting the mental health needs of the unhoused. Plus, is the concept of imposter syndrome masking inequity in the workplace? And, South Arkansas residents express their hopes and concerns about lithium mining in their town.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan explores a beautiful orange grove while giving a tour on a school bus monster truck, pampers some pals at a European beer spa, and works the late-shift to make the best Philly cheesesteak outside the City of Brotherly Love.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Seasonal ingredients are the stars of any dish, and Chef Chris Barnum-Dann and Chef Brett Bohlmann prove it in this fully vegetarian episode. Will the judges fawn over their flavors, or will they miss the meat?

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Can Chef Peter Kusiw of La Provence trip up Chef Nathanial Zimet of Boucherie with his secret sauce? Maybe yes, maybe no, but either way the mystery guest judge will make you question what you see.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Cyber-burglars, a dangerous mix of con artist and geek, wreak havoc on the technologically vulnerable. Our experts offer tips to spot such high-tech trickery before it's too late.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Explore the lives of extraordinary seniors who don't let age define them. From setting records to achieving lifelong dreams, this episode of Stitch celebrates those continuing to push the boundaries and pursue passions in their golden years.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: The best stories can transform us into magical worlds. They can also provide comfort or challenge us to learn new perspectives. This episode highlights authors, librarians and book lovers alike who make the world a better place.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Two serial killers whose chilling crimes left their communities devastated.

programme.title: My Amazing Cheap Date
programme.desc: Two couples rekindle their love of Orlando on dates that take them from museums featuring military machinery and unicorns, to twice-fried Korean fried chicken, to a cookie speakeasy.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Dreams come true while visiting a long-time fan and cancer survivor on their way to Louisville. Ryan wears a blindfold during a batting cage challenge with Jimmy, and a couple living behind the batting cage receive help getting back on their feet.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: While dedicating her time to criminal justice reform, Erinn placed connecting with nature on the back burner. She’s a free spirit who travels the world and jumps out of planes but doesn’t know what Kentucky has to offer – it's time to change that.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: Dancer. Educator. Diplomat. Peacemaker. Aysha Upchurch is a force of nature. But it’s her gift for inspiring students that makes her the real star.

programme.title: Restaurants on the Radar
programme.desc: Customizable sandwiches and crispy alligator bites? Orlando’s food scene has it all and avid foodie Brendan O’Connor is taking you to all the best spots on his radar, from cat cafes to Florida’s finest dining destinations.

programme.title: Maddy & Dixie Take Sactown
programme.desc: Follow Maddy & Dixie as they go on a historical tour around iconic Sacramento locales. Learn how the city was raised during floods. And check out the famous banana cream pie at Frank Fats.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Explore the lives of extraordinary seniors who don't let age define them. From setting records to achieving lifelong dreams, this episode of Stitch celebrates those continuing to push the boundaries and pursue passions in their golden years.

programme.title: Lowrider Legacy
programme.desc: Lowrider Legacy follows celebrated lowrider families, scholars, and activists and documents the history of resistance on wheels that has defined lowrider culture.

programme.title: Lost Innocence: The Tragedy of Harmony Montgomery
programme.desc: A police investigation into a missing seven-year-old girl in Manchester, New Hampshire reveals harrowing details that shock the state. But even more disturbing is the discovery that the tragic outcome could have been avoided.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Stories of kids and teens persevering through adversity.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Years ago Chef Bergen Carmen from Meril and Chef Aaron Villeda from Bao Mi worked side-by-side; now they’re back getting their hands dirty for some friendly competition. Will his old pal chicken liver turn out to be Chef Villeda’s ultimate downfall?

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Social butterfly, Metzli is ready for the next phase of her life when it comes to love. Will she grow with Matt, shake things up with Greg or harness a connection with David?

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A serial killer preys on women from a hardscrabble section of Daytona Beach, Florida. Detectives have boxes of DNA and ballistic evidence. They just don't have a suspect.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Every little bit helps! Justin and Ryan prove how true that is by throwing a fundraiser to help a woman in Maine get to have a place to call her own.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: In Kansas City, you can’t say tattoos without saying the name Wes Grimm. For three tattoo artists, he’s not just a name — he’s also a judge. Can these artists design a tattoo worthy of the name, and who will have just enough heart to win?

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: Ashley meets an Oklahoma DIY-woodworker and a New Orleans spooky miniaturist.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories
programme.desc: From a skier who turned her tragedy into the state's biggest charity event, to a cyclist who energizes others with maple syrup, you can't take the "Vermont" out of Burlington athletes.

programme.title: My Amazing Cheap Date
programme.desc: On two blind dates in Orlando, local couples cozy up over bird watching, spy friendly gators, sample decadent desserts, and get loose on game night.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: From burlesque to roller skating to a Titanic museum, our local Orlando experts won’t lead you astray. We’ve got rock stars and drag queens who are passionate about ‘The City Beautiful’ and know exactly which spots are worth your time.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: From fencing, to ninja warrior training, to boxing and Tai Chi, Rob discovers an inner strength he never knew he had…while getting kicked in the keister.

programme.title: Julia Tries Everything
programme.desc: Bread bowls, mac and cheese and much more are on the menu for Julia at Panera.

programme.title: Silver Linings
programme.desc: Welcome to Lancaster, PA, one of the country's hottest retirement hubs. Here, seniors, from a beauty pageant queen to a 90 year-old swim champion aren’t slowing down; they’re rewriting the playbook and redefining aging in America.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Stitch recognizes the feel-good, do-good and all-around good from across the country. This episode shares stories of Americans spreading kindness, from kids making a difference to stories of people helping veterans in need.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Aaron Poret of Bywater American Bistro goes head to head with Chef Jas Rogers, Owner and Creator of Saucex Dat. Will fried dough foil Chef Poret’s chances of success, or will he rise to Chef Rogers’ challenge?

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Randi, a 29-year-old single from Omaha, is ready to date three lovely ladies. Will she find a connection with Karen, will Leia pull all the right heartstrings or will things get spicy with Emily?

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: These days, cameras are everywhere. Those up to no good can run, but Big Brother is always watching.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: David suffered from a blood disorder and hasn’t had a full set of teeth since he was 12. Heather struggles with a rare genetic condition that put her in dentures in her 20s. Can the FIXT team write the final chapter in their health journeys?

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Angel captures a different angle on Baltimore with activist and photojournalist, Devin Allen. They explore street photography, pop wheelies with a cutting-edge community dirt biking program and enjoy Charm City's most decadent treats.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan goes underwater with dolphins and sting rays to learn about ocean conservation, tests his construction skills against a LEGO Master Builder, and preps a theme park dedicated to one of children's most beloved TV characters, Peppa Pig!

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Chris has an unstoppable spirit, despite the challenges of Down syndrome. His resilience has propelled him to extraordinary achievements, including clinching the gold in Greece. Meaghan takes him on a whirlwind of experiences tailored just for him.

programme.title: The Roller Coaster Wars That Birthed a Beast

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers

programme.title: Stitch

programme.title: FIXT

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
